title: sideroxylon marginatum
intro: Sideroxylon marginatum, also known as the White Ironwood or White Beech, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Australia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, Sideroxylon marginatum can be found in the form of a tincture, decoction, or salve, which are used to apply its medicinal properties topically or take internally to reap its health benefits.
